reasoning: Singapore is one of the safest nations globally, has no active insurgencies, low crime rates, and extreme stability. The ACLED reference value is 1.0 (implying a 30-day average of exactly 0 prior events). Resolving to 'yes' requires 11 or more 'Violence against civilians' events (organized armed group inflicting violence on unarmed non-combatants) within a 30-day window. Judicial punishment and routine law enforcement do not normally meet this definition, and even if they did, 11 executions/canings in a 30-day window is historically unprecedented. There have been no signs of sudden unrest or terrorism in Singapore as of June 2025. Given the minimum recommended probability limit around 0.02-0.05, I am setting the forecast near the floor, with slight uncertainty scaling for later resolution dates.
